summary.intent: Investigate GitHub issue #705 reporting that Docker sandbox containers can read host metadata from /proc and /sys, and implement a fix covering both Docker and Podman backends.
summary.outcome: Added hostname isolation and tmpfs masks for sensitive /sys subtrees in hardening_args(), updated tests and docs, and committed/pushed changes with a PR created.

repo: Docker and Podman share a single DockerSandbox implementation — fixes to hardening_args() apply to both runtimes automatically.
Apple Container backend is unaffected by /proc and /sys leaks because it uses a full VM with its own kernel.
deny_paths config only controls Moltis's file-access tool layer, not what the container kernel exposes directly to shell commands.

code.finding: hardening_args() is the single place to add Docker/Podman container hardening flags; already had --cap-drop ALL, --security-opt no-new-privileges, and read-only root but was missing hostname isolation and /sys tmpfs masks.
Hardening tests enumerate expected flags explicitly and needed updating whenever hardening_args() grows new entries.

workflow: Beads issue tracker is used to claim and close work items during the session.
The project uses worktrees under ~/.superset/worktrees/moltis/ for parallel development branches.
Standard flow: build test → cargo fmt check → clippy → close issue → commit/push/PR.

friction: Initial attempt to check the GitHub issue failed with a 401 authentication error and required a retry.
open_items: /proc still exposes some host metadata (kernel version, CPU info, memory layout, cgroup paths) — only /sys hardware identifier paths were masked in this fix.
A custom seccomp or AppArmor profile was noted as a stronger long-term fix but was not implemented.
Docker --cgroupns=private was mentioned as a potential improvement but not applied.
